formula_note: Direct civilian deaths come straight from B'Tselem's 'did not take part in hostilities' category.
quality_note: Best available civilian death count for the episode.

formula_note: Direct combatant deaths come from B'Tselem's 'took part in hostilities' category.
uncertainty_note: Strong numerator, but narrower than some broader armed-force estimates.
quality_note: Best available combatant death count for the episode.

formula_note: Unknown-status deaths = 248 police officers + 36 unresolved-status deaths.
uncertainty_note: Large enough to matter; keeping it separate is essential.
quality_note: Conservative treatment that avoids silent police allocation.

formula_note: Auxiliary child deaths from the B'Tselem civilian subset.
uncertainty_note: No additional denominator estimate attempted for children.

formula_note: Auxiliary women deaths from the B'Tselem civilian subset.
Population interpolation between PCBS mid-2008 (1.4m) and mid-2009 (1.5m), multiplied by 23/30.44 month-equivalents for the war phase.
uncertainty_note: Population uncertainty is modest relative to combatant-force uncertainty.
quality_note: The full Gaza Strip population is used because the enclosed strip was the entire civilian theater.

formula_note: Low uses 2,000 core Qassam combatants. Best uses an explicit midpoint interpolation to 8,750. High uses a 15,500 broad Hamas military/paramilitary/police-inclusive ceiling. Each is multiplied by 23/30.44.
uncertainty_note: This denominator is the dominant uncertainty driver, and the high end is broader than the strict combatant-death numerator.
quality_note: Publishable only as a wide-band estimate.

formula_note: dr_v1_direct = (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months) / (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months). Low uses high civilian exposure with low combatant exposure; high uses low civilian exposure with high combatant exposure. Recomputed from component estimates under current DR validation rules.
uncertainty_note: Interpretation is highly denominator-sensitive because combatant-force size is uncertain and police deaths are excluded from the combatant numerator.
Adult-female share is inferred from Gaza total sex shares plus a Gaza-specific child sex split assumption.
quality_note: Good enough for a publishable women-relative-risk layer because the death category is explicitly adult women.

uncertainty_note: Uses Gaza under-18 share reported for mid-2009.
quality_note: Directly matched to an under-18 death category.

uncertainty_note: Adult-male share is inferred from Gaza total sex shares plus a Gaza-specific child sex split assumption.
quality_note: Residual denominator is supportable because women are over 18 and children are under 18.

uncertainty_note: Safe residual because the source categories are explicitly adult women over 18 and children under 18.
quality_note: Non-overlap is explicit in the source table.

formula_note: ((adult male civilian deaths / adult male person-months) / (adult women deaths / women person-months)).
uncertainty_note: Driven mainly by the inferred adult-female and adult-male baseline shares; fatality categories themselves are explicit.
quality_note: wc_dr_women > 1 indicates adult male civilians died at a higher rate than adult women in this episode.

formula_note: ((adult male civilian deaths / adult male person-months) / (child deaths / child person-months)).
uncertainty_note: Adult-male denominator is inferred; child numerator and denominator are directly age-matched to under-18.
quality_note: wc_dr_children_u18 > 1 indicates adult male civilians died at a higher rate than children in this episode.
Below 1 means children were a smaller share of civilian deaths than of the Gaza population.

uncertainty_note: Uses the derived adult-female population share to match the adult-women death category.
quality_note: Below 1 means adult women were a smaller share of civilian deaths than of the adult female population.
